title: Dispatch task execution isolation to fix main-context blowup

content.title: Dispatch task execution isolation to fix main-context blowup
content.summary: build-code executes every task's TDD cycle inline in the main conversation thread, so all tool output (Read/Edit/Bash/test loops) accumulates in the main context window. On newer token-hungry models this blows up the main context on every dispatch run of a 6-11 task file, which is expensive and breaks the user's AFK tooling. It was not a problem on prior leaner models. The fix is to isolate per-task execution into ephemeral build-runner subagents that return only compact receipts to the main thread. dispatch-tasks stays a thin router, build-code becomes a lean per-task orchestrator loop holding only receipts, full traces go to disk logs referenced by path, and the AFK loop skips leaf failures, defers HITL, and halts on blocker failure, resuming for free via task JSON status.
content.problem_statement: build-code runs each task's /tdd cycle inline in the main thread. Every tool call, test run, and file edit stays in the main conversation context. Token-hungry sonnet-5-class models turned this latent design flaw into main-context blowup on every dispatch of a 6-11 task file. Cost rises and the user's AFK tooling breaks. The same design ran fine on leaner sonnet-4.6-era models.
content.solution: Move per-task execution out of the main thread into isolated single-task executor subagents (build-runner). Each executor runs one task's full TDD cycle, spawns the helper runners, writes its full trace to a disk log, and returns only a compact receipt. dispatch-tasks remains the router. build-code becomes the per-task orchestrator loop that gates on blocked_by, spawns one executor per task, and holds only the tiny receipts. Receipts are written back into the task JSON; traces live in Markdown logs referenced by log_path and read only on failure.
decisions: Move build-code task execution out of the main thread into isolated subagents so only compact receipts return to main. This is the foundational fix; context-budget pausing was rejected in favor of isolation.

Use fine-grained per-task isolation: one isolated executor subagent per task. Coarse per-branch and batched-coarse isolation were rejected because 6-11 task files of heavy TDD cycles would overflow a single subagent and 38 percent blocked_by density makes batching fiddly.

Build a shared context brief once per run via the context-loader agent and pass it to each executor. Thread each completed task's receipt forward as a breadcrumb to cut re-discovery cost of per-task cold starts.
The orchestrator loop gates task selection on blocked_by: pick the next not_started task whose blockers are all done or merged, spawn one executor, collect the receipt, write status, repeat.

dispatch-tasks stays a thin router, unchanged. It remains re-entrant, one branch per invocation, partitioning by task_type and branch. It was renamed from run-tasks precisely because tasks became varied (code plus triage tickets).

build-code becomes the per-task orchestrator loop. It stops running /tdd inline, spawns one build-runner executor per task, and stays in the main thread holding only receipts, never the fat TDD traces.

Create a new agent named build-runner as the isolated per-task executor. It runs one task's full TDD cycle, spawns lint-runner/test-runner/browser-checker, writes the trace log, and returns a compact receipt.

build-runner uses model sonnet (tier T3), uniform across all tasks, matching current build-code and /tdd. No per-task model downgrade. Downgrading the executor to haiku is false economy because a mis-tiered task could produce the blocker that halts the run.
Register build-runner by adding "build-runner": "T3" to the model-tiers.json agents map and running sync-model-tiers.py --apply, which stamps the model into the agent .md and updates the registry.
Fix the stale "spawned by run-tasks" references in lint-runner/test-runner/browser-checker to point at build-runner, since run-tasks is the former name of dispatch-tasks.
The structured receipt is written back into the task JSON item, reusing existing status/pr/follow_ups fields and adding four optional additive fields: summary, files_touched, tests, log_path.

The full per-task execution trace is written to a Markdown log at docs/tasks/.logs/<taskfile-basename>/<task_id>.md, referenced by log_path. Main reads it only on failure or explicit request. JSON is not used for the trace to avoid escaping and bloat.
Add the four receipt fields to task-schema.json as optional (not required) additive properties. Keep additionalProperties:false. Do not bump schema_version because the change is backward-compatible.

handoff, task-producing skills, and other task-JSON consumers need no changes because the new fields are optional. handoff in particular produces Markdown, not JSON, so it is entirely unaffected. Blast radius is the schema file plus build-code (loop writer) plus the new build-runner executor.
Trace logs are transient debug artifacts cleaned by clean-scaffolding, the same treatment as browser-checks (deleted, not archived).

AFK loop obstacle policy: a leaf task failure marks the task failed, skips it, and continues. A blocker task failure (something has it in blocked_by) halts the whole run. A HITL task is marked deferred_hitl and the loop continues independent work, never halting on it.

A blocker-failure halt is treated as a scoping signal. The end-of-run report frames it as a cue to re-grill or re-seed because it likely means dependencies or requirements were mis-scoped or scope crept, not a cue to blindly retry.

Blocker failure halts the whole run, not just the failed task's dependent subtree. Subtree-only halt was rejected because the user's task files are almost always a single dependency chain, so whole-run halt is simpler with rare downside.
The loop produces an end-of-run consolidated report listing done, failed, deferred_hitl, and blocked tasks, each with its log_path.
Resumability is free via the task JSON status field as the checkpoint. Re-invoking dispatch-tasks/build-code picks up not_started and failed tasks. No handoff or checkpoint protocol is needed.

Drop the context-budget / token-threshold auto-pause idea entirely. Isolation plus status-based resumability makes it obsolete. The user's original 'pause at a threshold' hypothesis was correct for the inline world but is now moot.

Task-file size is decoupled from context management. Files can grow to match one ticket's scope because main only ever holds receipts. The granularity guard moves down to per-task sizing: each task is a clean vertical slice sized to one TDD cycle.
Scoping model: 1 ticket = 1 task file = 1 encapsulated unit of work = 1 PR. If decomposition balloons, the original ticket was too big and should be split into sub-tickets upstream.

out_of_scope.text: Grill-time ticket-scope check that flags an oversized ticket during grilling and splits it into sub-tickets before seed/task generation.
out_of_scope.rationale: Explicitly excluded, not even deferred. It is upstream of the execution redesign and touches grill-me/grill-with-docs/to-tasks, which would widen blast radius on otherwise cohesive work.

out_of_scope.text: Per-task model tiering (haiku for simple tasks, sonnet for complex).
out_of_scope.rationale: Task complexity is not reliably encoded in the schema, and a mis-tiered haiku task could produce the blocker that halts the run. build-runner stays uniform sonnet.

out_of_scope.text: Coarse per-branch isolation and batched-coarse isolation of task execution.
out_of_scope.rationale: 6-11 task files of heavy TDD cycles would overflow a single coarse subagent, and batching across 38 percent blocked_by density is fiddly. Fine per-task isolation was chosen instead.

out_of_scope.text: Context-budget / token-threshold auto-pause or auto-handoff mechanism in the main thread.
out_of_scope.rationale: Isolation plus JSON-status resumability removes the bloat this would defend against. Building it adds machinery to stop a fire that can no longer start.

implementation_decisions: task-schema.json: add summary, files_touched, tests, log_path as optional properties on the task item. Do not add them to required[]. Keep additionalProperties:false. Do not bump schema_version.
build-runner registration: add "build-runner": "T3" to model-tiers.json agents map, then run ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/sync-model-tiers.py --apply.
build-code runs context-loader once per invocation to build the shared brief, then passes it to every build-runner spawn.
clean-scaffolding deletes docs/tasks/.logs trace artifacts the same way it deletes browser-checks (no archive).
build-code loop stays in the main thread; only build-runner executor contexts are isolated and discarded.
testing_decisions: No unit tests for this work; it is claude-code-shared infra (skills, agents, scripts, schema). Validate the schema change with validate-schema.sh and verify behavior with manual dispatch runs against a real multi-task file.
Verify nested spawn depth works: build-code (main) -> build-runner (agent) -> lint-runner/test-runner/browser-checker (agents). Confirm depth-2 agent spawning is permitted before relying on it.
risks_and_tradeoffs: Per-task cold-start cost: each executor re-derives some context. Mitigated by the shared brief and receipt breadcrumbs, but total token spend is higher than inline warm context. Accepted because the cost lands in discarded executor contexts, not the main window.
Loss of live play-by-play in the main thread. Mitigated by the log_path trace pulled only on failure.
Nested spawn depth of 2 or more (build-code -> build-runner -> helper runners). export-tasks -> export-tasks-gh proves one level; depth 2 must be verified before relying on it.
Whole-run halt on blocker failure loses AFK throughput on multi-chain files. Accepted because the user's files are almost always a single dependency chain.
success_metrics: Main-thread context after a full dispatch run stays roughly flat regardless of task count; only compact receipts accumulate.
A 9-11 task file dispatches AFK without main-context blowup.
Code quality does not regress because build-runner stays on sonnet.
further_notes: run-tasks is the former name of dispatch-tasks. It was renamed when tasks became varied (code plus triage tickets), which is why some agent docs still say 'spawned by run-tasks'.

Root cause of the blowup: build-code ran /tdd inline in the main thread. Token-hungry sonnet-5-class models turned a latent design flaw into main-context blowup. The same design ran fine on sonnet-4.6-era models.
content.next_action: Run /to-tasks on this seed to break the dispatch execution-isolation redesign into implementable tasks.

tasks.title: Extend task-schema with receipt fields and run statuses
tasks.description: Add the per-task execution receipt to task-schema.json. Add four optional properties to the task item: summary (string), files_touched (array of strings), tests (object capturing passed/failed counts), and log_path (string pointing at the trace log). None go into the task-item required[] array. Keep additionalProperties:false on the task item. Do NOT bump schema_version (stays '2'; the change is backward-compatible additive). Also extend the task-item status enum with two new values the AFK loop needs: 'failed' (a build-runner task that did not pass) and 'deferred_hitl' (a HITL task skipped for a later interactive pass). Characterization first: capture that existing task files without the new fields still validate, then make the change keeping that true.
acceptance_criteria: task-schema.json task item exposes summary, files_touched, tests, log_path as optional properties (absent from the item required[] array).
task-item status enum includes failed and deferred_hitl in addition to the existing not_started, in_progress, done, merged, blocked.
additionalProperties:false is retained on the task item and schema_version const stays '2' (no bump).

Verification via validate-schema.sh: a sample task WITH the four new fields and a failed/deferred_hitl status validates, and an existing task WITHOUT the new fields still validates (backward compatible).
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "Extend task-schema with receipt fields and run statuses" --body "Adds summary/files_touched/tests/log_path optional fields and failed/deferred_hitl statuses to task-schema.json task items. Backward compatible, schema_version stays 2."

tasks.title: Create build-runner executor agent and register at tier T3
tasks.description: Create a new agent build-runner (claude-code-shared/agents/build-runner.md) that executes exactly ONE task end-to-end: seed and run the /tdd red-green-refactor cycle for that single task, spawn lint-runner and test-runner, and spawn browser-checker when the task carries browser_verify. It writes the FULL execution trace to docs/tasks/.logs/<taskfile-basename>/<task_id>.md and returns a compact receipt carrying status, summary, files_touched, tests (passed/failed), pr, log_path, and any follow_ups. It must never loop over multiple tasks; one task per spawn. Register it by adding "build-runner": "T3" to the model-tiers.json agents map, then run sync-model-tiers.py --apply so model sonnet is stamped into the agent .md and the registry is updated.
acceptance_criteria: claude-code-shared/agents/build-runner.md exists and runs exactly one task's TDD cycle, spawning lint-runner/test-runner and browser-checker (only when browser_verify is present).
build-runner writes the full trace to docs/tasks/.logs/<taskfile-basename>/<task_id>.md and returns a compact receipt with status, summary, files_touched, tests, pr, log_path, follow_ups.
model-tiers.json agents map contains build-runner:T3 and sync-model-tiers.py --apply has been run.
Verification: after sync-model-tiers.py --apply, build-runner.md frontmatter and the agent registry both show model sonnet.
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "Create build-runner executor agent and register at T3" --body "Adds claude-code-shared/agents/build-runner.md, a single-task TDD executor that spawns lint-runner/test-runner/browser-checker and writes a full trace to docs/tasks/.logs/, returning a compact receipt. Registers build-runner in model-tiers.json and agents/registry.json at T3 (sonnet); sync-model-tiers.py --apply confirms model sonnet in both."

tasks.title: Rewrite build-code as per-task orchestrator loop
tasks.description: Replace build-code's inline /tdd execution with a per-task orchestrator loop that stays in the main thread and holds only compact receipts. Characterization first: before restructuring, capture build-code's current control flow (blocker checks, status writeback, pr writeback, follow_ups append) so the new loop preserves that behavior. Then: on start, build a shared context brief ONCE via the context-loader agent. Loop: pick the next not_started task whose blocked_by are all done or merged, spawn ONE build-runner for it, collect the receipt, write the receipt fields (status, summary, files_touched, tests, log_path, follow_ups) back into the task JSON, thread each completed receipt forward as a breadcrumb into subsequent build-runner spawns, and repeat. build-code must never hold the full TDD traces, only the receipts.
acceptance_criteria: build-code no longer runs /tdd inline; it spawns exactly one build-runner per task and retains only receipts in the main thread.
A shared brief is built once via context-loader at loop start and passed to each build-runner; each completed receipt is threaded forward as a breadcrumb.
Receipt fields are written back into the task JSON item after each build-runner returns; blocked_by gating selects the next runnable task.
Verification: a manual dispatch run of a multi-task all-AFK file completes with each task isolated, receipts and log_path written into the JSON, and traces written under docs/tasks/.logs/.
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "Rewrite build-code as per-task orchestrator loop" --body "build-code no longer runs /tdd inline. It builds a shared context brief once via context-loader, then spawns exactly one build-runner per task, writing back the returned receipt (status, summary, files_touched, tests, log_path) and threading each completed receipt forward as a breadcrumb. build-code retains only receipts in the main thread; full traces live under docs/tasks/.logs/. Note: could not live-fire a build-runner spawn in this session because the harness's agent registry loads once at session start and does not pick up a newly created agent file mid-session; verified structurally (frontmatter, registry entries, control-flow review) instead. Recommend a smoke run after restarting the session."

tasks.title: Add AFK obstacle policy and end-of-run report to build-code
tasks.description: Extend the build-code loop with obstacle handling and a final report. A leaf task failure (no other task lists it in blocked_by) marks the task status failed, skips it, and continues. A blocker task failure (at least one task lists it in blocked_by) halts the whole run; the report frames the halt as a scoping signal recommending re-grill or re-seed, not a blind retry. A HITL-type task is marked deferred_hitl and the loop continues independent work without halting on it. At the end, emit a consolidated report listing done, failed, deferred_hitl, and blocked tasks, each with its log_path. Resumption is free via task JSON status: re-invoking the loop picks up not_started and failed tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Leaf task failure sets status failed, skips, and continues; blocker task failure halts the whole run with a report framing it as a scoping signal (re-grill/re-seed).
A HITL-type task is marked deferred_hitl and the loop continues independent work without halting.
End-of-run report lists done/failed/deferred_hitl/blocked tasks, each with its log_path; re-invoking resumes not_started and failed tasks.
Verification: manual runs injecting (1) a leaf failure, (2) a blocker failure, and (3) a HITL task each exercise the correct policy branch.
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "Add AFK obstacle policy and end-of-run report to build-code" --body "Leaf task failures set status failed, skip, and continue; blocker task failures halt the run with a report framed as a scoping signal recommending re-grill/re-seed instead of blind retry. HITL tasks are marked deferred_hitl and never halt independent work. The queue-builder now resumes not_started and failed tasks on re-invoke. End-of-run report lists done/failed/deferred_hitl/blocked with each task's log_path. Note: same session constraint as T-0072, agent-spawn branches were verified structurally via control-flow review rather than a live multi-outcome run; recommend a smoke run after restarting the session to exercise leaf-failure, blocker-failure, and HITL branches live."

tasks.title: Clean trace logs in clean-scaffolding
tasks.description: Extend the clean-scaffolding skill to delete docs/tasks/.logs trace artifacts, treating them like browser-checks (delete, do not archive). Archiving of seeds, PRDs, tasks, and handoffs is unchanged. Characterization first: capture clean-scaffolding's current archive-vs-delete behavior so only the new .logs deletion is added.
acceptance_criteria: clean-scaffolding deletes docs/tasks/.logs trace artifacts without archiving, matching the browser-checks handling.
Existing archive behavior for seeds, PRDs, tasks, and handoffs is unchanged.
Verification: running clean-scaffolding on a tree containing docs/tasks/.logs removes the logs and leaves the docs/archive contents intact.
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "Clean trace logs in clean-scaffolding" --body "Extends clean-scaffolding to delete docs/tasks/.logs trace artifacts as a delete-set (no archiving), matching browser-checks handling. Existing archive behavior unchanged."

tasks.title: Fix stale run-tasks references in helper runner agents
tasks.description: Update the 'spawned by run-tasks' references in the lint-runner, test-runner, and browser-checker agent docs to point at build-runner, since run-tasks is the former name of dispatch-tasks and these helpers are now spawned by the build-runner executor. Update e2e-runner's spawner note to build-runner as well if it references run-tasks.
acceptance_criteria: lint-runner, test-runner, and browser-checker docs reference build-runner as their spawner, not run-tasks.
Verification: grep for 'spawned by run-tasks' across claude-code-shared/agents returns no stale results.
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title "Fix stale run-tasks references in helper runner agents" --body "Updates browser-checker, lint-runner, and test-runner agent docs to reference build-runner as their spawner instead of the former run-tasks name. e2e-runner had no run-tasks reference to fix."
